1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

Both custom targets in `IncrementalBroken.csproj` will re-run on every build because they lack `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, which MSBuild needs to determine if a target is up-to-date.

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild always runs it.
- Uses `DateTime.Now`, so the written file **always changes**, which would also invalidate any downstream caching regardless.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild always runs it.
- `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` **updates the file's timestamp even when content is identical**, forcing `CoreCompile` to re-run every time since it sees a "newer" input.
- Runs `git rev-parse HEAD` on every build even when the commit hasn't changed.

### Impact
Together these force a full recompilation on every `dotnet build`, even when no source files changed.

### Recommended Fixes
| Target | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Remove it or gate it behind a flag (timestamps are inherently non-incremental). |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Add `Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFile)"` / `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"`, and use a hash-comparison check so the file is only rewritten when the content actually changes. |

Would you like me to apply these fixes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I can analyze the incremental build issues in this project. Let me identify the problems:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp` target** - This target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` and writes the current timestamp to a file. Since it has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, MSBuild will run it every single time. Moreover, the timestamp changes every time (`DateTime.Now`), which means even if MSBuild could skip it, the file content changes every build, potentially invalidating downstream targets.

2. **`EmbedGitHash` target** - This target also runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` with no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes. 

The real problem is that both targets execute unconditionally on every build because they lack the incremental build metadata. The `EmbedGitHash` target runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes the result to a generated source file, but even though the hash stays the same between commits, `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` updates the file timestamp regardless, forcing unnecessary recompilation.
